Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltfs Discovery Requests And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Frank Cialone, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff/moving party David E. Smith. Lawrence Calaghan, Esq., appearing for defendant/opposing party David Newlin.
Ruling:
Hearing held. Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintif David Smith's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests is granted in part, denied in part, and moot in part. As supplemental responses have been made, the motion is moot as the court will not determine their adequacy on this motion. However, the parties must be guided by the rulings made in this order.
As to the Special Interrogatories, the general objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Any claim of a right against self-incrimination must be asserted with particularity to individual discovery requests.
As to Nos. 1-17, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendants are ordered to respond further. However, the responses may be adequate if they were identify the documents not in Defendants' possession which would provide the necessary information. However, the court cannot order a further response where it is alleged that a complete response cannot be made after reasonable search and diligent inquiry.
As to the Requests For Admission, the general objections are overruled and ordered stricken. As to Nos. 1-17, the objections
are overruled and ordered stricken. However, the court cannot order a further response where it is alleged that there is insufficient information to admit or deny. The portion of the responses that state that discovery and investigation are continuing is ordered stricken.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-27-2007 Continued To Apr-25-07 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212
